description: Fix UX issues in the side panel: vertically center search icons, redesign PluginIcon to show ready/not-ready states with filled vs outlined SVGs, move version+trust into a Tooltip on icon hover, remove tab state label (icon conveys state), show tool description as primary text instead of technical name, align all switches to the same column, fix AccordionContent dark mode colors, and verify all components match RetroUI source exactly. ALL code must follow React best practices: no hacky workarounds, no magic numbers, no translate-y pixel hacks, no inline styles where Tailwind classes exist, proper component composition, derived state instead of useEffect for state sync, clean prop interfaces, and semantic HTML.

userStories.title: Fix search input icon vertical centering
userStories.description: As a user, I want the search icon and clear button to be perfectly vertically centered in the input
acceptanceCriteria: The Search icon is vertically centered within the input field
The X clear button is vertically centered within the input field
The translate-y-[4px] hack is removed from both icons
The vertical centering uses only top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 (standard CSS centering)
Both icons remain correctly positioned when the input has content vs empty
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes: In App.tsx, the Search icon has classes 'absolute top-1/2 left-5 h-4 w-4 -translate-y-1/2 translate-y-[4px]'. The translate-y-[4px] is a hack that overrides proper centering. Remove it. The correct approach is: make the parent wrapper a flex container with items-center, OR use the standard absolute centering pattern 'absolute top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2' without any pixel overrides. If the input height doesn't match, fix the input/wrapper sizing properly -- never add pixel offsets to compensate. Consider extracting a SearchInput component if the search markup is getting complex. No magic numbers, no pixel hacks.

userStories.title: Redesign PluginIcon with ready/not-ready dual-state SVGs
userStories.description: As a user, I want the plugin icon to visually indicate whether the plugin is ready or not, using a filled icon for ready and an outlined icon for not-ready
acceptanceCriteria: PluginIcon.tsx accepts a new prop: ready: boolean
When ready=true, the SVG puzzle piece is filled/solid (stroke + fill)
When ready=false (closed or unavailable), the SVG puzzle piece is outlined only (stroke, no fill)
Both states use the same puzzle-piece shape -- only fill differs
The filled version uses currentColor for both stroke and fill
The outlined version uses currentColor for stroke and 'none' or 'transparent' for fill
The 32x32 container still has border-2 border-border bg-muted
The ready state uses text-foreground for a bold look
The not-ready state uses text-muted-foreground for a muted/dim look
The pluginName prop is retained for future real icon mapping
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes: Update PluginIcon.tsx. Add ready: boolean prop. The SVG should be a recognizable puzzle piece. For ready: fill='currentColor' stroke='currentColor'. For not-ready: fill='none' stroke='currentColor'. Add a color class: ready uses text-foreground (black/white), not-ready uses text-muted-foreground (gray). The container keeps the same border treatment. In PluginCard.tsx, pass ready={plugin.tabState === 'ready'} to PluginIcon.

userStories.title: Move version and trust tier into Tooltip on plugin icon hover
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see version and trust info by hovering the plugin icon, not as inline badges cluttering the card
acceptanceCriteria: The version Badge is removed from the visible plugin card header row
The trust tier Badge is removed from the visible plugin card header row
The tab state Badge is removed from the visible plugin card header row (icon conveys ready/not-ready)
Hovering the PluginIcon shows a RetroUI Tooltip with text like 'v0.3.1 · local'
The Tooltip uses RetroUI Tooltip component (variant='default': border-2 border-border bg-background)
The plugin card header row is simplified to: [PluginIcon] [plugin name] [chevron] ... [switch]
The layout is cleaner and less cluttered with just the icon, name, and switch visible

bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
In PluginCard.tsx: (1) Remove all three Badge components from the header row (version, tabState, trustTier). (2) Wrap PluginIcon in a Tooltip:
v{plugin.version} · {plugin.trustTier}. (3) The header row becomes: [icon-with-tooltip] [name] [chevron] [switch]. Much cleaner. Import { Tooltip } from './retro/Tooltip.js'. Remove the now-unused tabStateBadgeVariant and trustBadgeVariant helper functions. Clean up all unused imports (Badge may still be used for toggle errors, verify). Follow the principle: if code is unused, delete it.

userStories.title: Show tool description as primary text, tool name in Tooltip
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see human-readable tool descriptions instead of technical names like edit_message
acceptanceCriteria: ToolRow shows the tool description as the primary text (text-xs font-medium text-foreground)
The technical tool name (e.g., edit_message) is no longer shown as visible text
Hovering the description text shows a RetroUI Tooltip with the technical tool name
If the description is too long, it truncates and the Tooltip shows the full description + tool name
The ToolIcon is still shown before the description text
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
In ToolRow.tsx, currently the layout is:
name
description (in tooltip)
. Change to: one line showing description as primary, with Tooltip showing the technical name. New structure:
{description}
 {name}
This is simpler: one line per tool, description is readable, technical name available on hover. Important: the Tooltip.Provider should ideally wrap at a higher level (e.g., PluginList or App) rather than creating one per ToolRow -- multiple nested Providers are wasteful. Move Tooltip.Provider up to the nearest shared parent. Follow React best practices: lift shared context providers up, don't duplicate per-item.

userStories.title: Align all switches (toggle-all and per-tool) to the same right column
userStories.description: As a user, I want the enable/disable all switch and individual tool switches to be visually aligned in a single right column
acceptanceCriteria: The toggle-all Switch in the plugin header is right-aligned to the same horizontal position as tool Switches
Tool Switches are right-aligned with consistent right padding
When the accordion is expanded, the toggle-all Switch and tool Switches form a clean vertical column on the right
The Switch column has consistent padding-right across header and tool rows
The alignment is maintained regardless of plugin name length or description length
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes: Currently the header Switch has px-3 on its wrapper div, and ToolRow has px-3 overall. The Switch in the header sits after the ChevronDown, while tool Switches are at the end of the ToolRow flex. To align: (1) Both the header and ToolRow should use the same right padding (e.g., pr-3). (2) The Switch should be the last flex item in both rows with the same alignment. (3) In the header, the structure is: [trigger: icon+name+chevron] [switch-wrapper]. In ToolRow: [icon] [text] [spinner?] [switch]. Both should have the Switch as the rightmost item with the same pr value. Use consistent padding values (not magic numbers). The alignment should be achieved through proper flex layout, not through absolute positioning or pixel offsets. If the AccordionContent has its own padding (px-4 pt-2 pb-4 from RetroUI source), the ToolRow padding must account for this so Switches align with the header Switch. Consider: the header has px-3 on the switch wrapper. AccordionContent has px-4. ToolRow has px-3. So ToolRow Switch is at (4 + 3) = 7 from left of card, while header Switch is at 3 from right. Use a shared padding constant or ensure both use the same right-edge offset.

userStories.title: Fix AccordionContent dark mode colors and verify all retro components match source
userStories.description: As a developer, I want AccordionContent to use theme tokens instead of hardcoded bg-white text-gray-700, and verify all RetroUI components match source
acceptanceCriteria: AccordionContent in retro/Accordion.tsx no longer has hardcoded 'bg-white text-gray-700'
AccordionContent uses 'bg-card text-card-foreground' instead (adapts to dark mode)
This is the ONE intentional deviation from RetroUI source -- justified because the side panel needs dark mode support and RetroUI's own source has this bug
All other retro/ component files have class strings identical to RetroUI source (no other deviations)
The cn() utility in lib/utils.ts uses the same logic as RetroUI source (clsx + twMerge)

bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes: In retro/Accordion.tsx, AccordionContent has: 'overflow-hidden font-body bg-white text-gray-700 data-[state=open]:animate-accordion-down data-[state=closed]:animate-accordion-up'. Change 'bg-white text-gray-700' to 'bg-card text-card-foreground'. This is the only intentional deviation from RetroUI source -- justified because the side panel needs dark mode support and RetroUI's source has hardcoded light-mode colors here. Do a full audit of all retro/ component files: read each one and compare class strings against ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/components/retroui/ source. Any unintentional deviation is a bug. Also verify lib/utils.ts matches RetroUI source. Follow project code quality rules: no TODO/FIXME comments, no hacky workarounds, proper typing on all props, named exports per convention.
